name: power-bi
description: Explore Power BI workspaces, datasets, and reports, run DAX queries, and trigger dataset refreshes

requiredApis.name: Power BI Service API (Azure app registration)

envVars.name: MICROSOFT_CLIENT_ID
envVars.description: Microsoft Azure App Client ID (Application ID)

tools.name: List Workspaces
tools.description: List Power BI workspaces (groups) the signed-in user has access to

tools.name: List My Workspace Datasets
tools.description: List datasets in the signed-in user's My workspace

tools.name: List Workspace Datasets
tools.description: List datasets in a specific Power BI workspace

tools.name: List My Workspace Reports
tools.description: List reports in the signed-in user's My workspace

tools.name: List Workspace Reports
tools.description: List reports in a specific Power BI workspace

tools.name: Execute DAX Query
tools.description: Run a read-only DAX query against a dataset and return the resulting rows

datasetId.description: Dataset ID (the dataset may live in My workspace or any workspace the user can access)

queries.description: Array with a single { query: '<DAX>' } object, e.g. [{ query: "EVALUATE TOPN(10, 'Sales')" }]. One query per call.

serializerSettings.description: Result serialization settings, e.g. { includeNulls: true }

impersonatedUserName.description: UPN of a user to impersonate for RLS-enabled models

tools.name: Refresh Dataset
tools.description: Trigger a data refresh for a dataset in the signed-in user's My workspace (returns 202 Accepted; the refresh runs asynchronously). Datasets in shared workspaces are not supported by this endpoint

datasetId.description: Dataset ID to refresh; the dataset must live in My workspace (workspace datasets use the groups refresh endpoint, which this tool does not call)

notifyOption.description: Mail notification option: NoNotification, MailOnFailure, or MailOnCompletion. Required by the API; on shared capacities this is the only field allowed.

tools.name: Refresh Workspace Dataset
tools.description: Trigger a data refresh for a dataset in a specific workspace (returns 202 Accepted; the refresh runs asynchronously)

groupId.description: Workspace (group) ID the dataset lives in, from List Workspaces

notifyOption.description: Mail notification option: NoNotification, MailOnFailure, or MailOnCompletion. Required by the API; on shared capacities this is the only field allowed.

tools.name: List Refresh History
tools.description: Get the refresh history of a dataset in the signed-in user's My workspace — the way to follow up the 202 returned by Refresh Dataset

datasetId.description: Dataset ID whose refresh history to fetch; the dataset must live in My workspace

$top.description: Number of most recent refresh entries to return (defaults to the last 60)

tools.name: List Workspace Refresh History
tools.description: Get the refresh history of a dataset in a specific workspace, including status and failure error codes

groupId.description: Workspace (group) ID the dataset lives in, from List Workspaces

datasetId.description: Dataset ID whose refresh history to fetch

$top.description: Number of most recent refresh entries to return (defaults to the last 60)

prompts.title: Workspace inventory
prompts.prompt: List my Power BI workspaces and the datasets and reports inside each one.

prompts.title: Run a DAX query
prompts.prompt: Run a DAX query against one of my Power BI datasets and summarize the result table.

prompts.title: Refresh a dataset
prompts.prompt: Trigger a refresh for my Power BI dataset and confirm it was accepted.

SETUP_GUIDE.title: Microsoft Power BI setup

steps.title: Get a Power BI account
steps.description: Sign in at https://app.powerbi.com with a work or school account. For testing, you can create a free Microsoft 365 developer tenant or start a Power BI trial and publish a sample report.

steps.title: Register an Azure app
steps.description: In the Azure portal, open Microsoft Entra ID > App registrations > New registration. Choose 'Accounts in any organizational directory' and add your callback URL as a Web redirect URI.

steps.title: Add Power BI delegated permissions
steps.description: Under API permissions, select Add a permission > Power BI Service > Delegated permissions, and add Workspace.Read.All, Dataset.Read.All, and Report.Read.All (plus Dataset.ReadWrite.All if you want to trigger refreshes).

steps.title: Create a client secret
steps.description: Under Certificates & secrets, create a client secret. Set MICROSOFT_CLIENT_ID to the Application (client) ID and MICROSOFT_CLIENT_SECRET to the secret value.

notes: Execute DAX Query requires the tenant setting 'Dataset Execute Queries REST API' (Power BI admin portal > Integration settings) to be enabled, and the user needs read and Build permissions on the dataset.
Execute DAX Query is limited to one query per call, 100,000 rows or 1,000,000 values, and 15 MB of data per query.
Dataset refreshes on shared capacities are limited to eight per day per dataset and only accept the notifyOption body field; Refresh Dataset needs the Dataset.ReadWrite.All scope.
Power BI REST APIs refer to workspaces as 'groups'.
